about.title: About
about.subtitle: App identity, version, system info, website, GitHub and License.

general.title: General
general.subtitle: Color & display, user profile, assistant and extension entries.

providers.title: Providers
providers.subtitle: Built-in templates, enabled state, Base URL, API paths, Response API, balance path and model configuration.
providers.name: Name
providers.get_key_title: Go to the provider's website to get an API key
providers.response_api_desc: When enabled, the chat endpoint switches to /responses
providers.history_reasoning_desc: Newer models (e.g. DeepSeek V4) require reasoning_content to be echoed back. When enabled, the reasoning_content of past assistant messages is sent with each request; models that don't need it ignore the field silently. Turn this off if a provider rejects requests over the unrecognized field.
providers.prompt_cache_desc: When enabled, adds cache_control to the system prompt and the second-to-last user message to reuse Anthropic's prompt cache.
providers.models_desc: Fetch the provider's models first, then check the ones to enable. {{count}} currently enabled.
providers.add_model_title: Add a model manually (for custom models not in the upstream list)
providers.no_models: No models yet. Click “Add model” to add one manually, or “Fetch models” to sync from upstream.
providers.tool_enabled: Tool calling is enabled — click to disable
providers.reasoning_enabled: Reasoning is enabled — click to disable
providers.test_model_ph: Fetch the model list or pick an enabled model first
providers.balance_desc: GETs the balance at the path below and reads the value from the specified JSON path.
providers.test_img_starting:
Saving config...
Running the image generation test...
providers.test_starting:
Saving config...
Reading the model list and preparing the test...
providers.models_read: Model list loaded, testing each entry...
providers.key_required_fetch: Fill in an API Key before fetching the model list
providers.key_required_enable: Fill in an API Key before enabling and fetching the model list
providers.no_models_enable: No models returned — can't enable. Check the provider config.
providers.enable_fetch_failed: Failed to fetch models, not enabled
providers.model_id_exists: A model with modelId "{{id}}" already exists — pick a different ID

assistants.title: Assistants
assistants.subtitle: Edit each assistant across basic, prompt, extensions, memory, request, MCP and local tools.
assistants.name: Name
assistants.message_template_desc: Wraps the user message before sending it to the model. Usually fine to leave as default.
assistants.template_missing_warn: The template doesn't contain {{token}}, so user messages won't be sent to the model.
assistants.preview_assistant_response: Hello, how can I help you?
assistants.preset_messages_desc: These preset messages are written when a new conversation is created, giving the assistant a stable opening context.
assistants.regex_desc: Runs substitutions after user input or assistant output; invalid regexes are skipped automatically.
assistants.max_tokens_desc: Leave blank to omit max_tokens for the provider.
assistants.memory_desc_post: , and saved memories are injected as a Memories prompt in later conversations.
assistants.memory_ph: Write a long-term memory, e.g. the user's preferred nickname, work background or long-term project setup
assistants.local_tools_desc: Local tool list. When enabled, they're injected only if the current model supports tool calls.

time_info.title: Time info
time_info.desc: Reads local PC time, timezone, weekday and timestamp.

js_engine.title: JavaScript engine
js_engine.desc: For calculations and plain-text data conversion. No DOM/Node API.

clipboard.title: Clipboard
clipboard.desc: Read/write the system clipboard; the model shouldn't write unless explicitly asked.

tts.title: Text to speech
tts.desc: Reads text aloud via the system voice.

ask_user.title: Ask user
ask_user.desc: Shows a question in the chat for the user to answer before continuing.

assistants.headers_desc: Appended to request headers when sending model requests.
assistants.bodies_desc: Merged into the request body by key; the value can be JSON or a plain string.

proxy.title: Proxy & port
proxy.subtitle: Set an HTTP proxy for all outbound traffic (AI APIs, search, MCP) and the local service port. Leave the proxy blank to follow the system proxy; leave the port blank to auto-select.
proxy.http_desc: All AI API requests are routed through this proxy
proxy.none_detected_desc: The system proxy is off, or the proxy tool hasn't started yet.
proxy.port_desc: Port the local service listens on, default 8080. Leave blank to auto-select; a fixed port is used when set, walking up if occupied. Takes effect after restart.
proxy.port_invalid: Port must be an integer between 1 and 65535
proxy.port_restart_note: Port changes take effect after restarting. The running instance still uses the old port.

stats.title: Statistics
stats.subtitle: Usage stats and an activity heatmap based on local chats, request logs and model usage.
stats.heatmap_empty: No chat data yet. The heatmap appears once you start chatting.

logs.title: Request logs
logs.subtitle: Endpoint, status code, duration and error summary for every provider request.

search.title: Search services
search.subtitle: Bing and Rikkahub are included by default, with Tavily, Exa, Zhipu and more as templates.
search.name: Name
search.testing_start: Saving config and running the search service test...

models.title: Default models & prompts
models.subtitle: Pick a default model and prompt for chat, titles, translation, suggestions, OCR, compression and more.
models.note: Only models that passed the provider test are shown here. Title, translation, suggestion, OCR and compression prompts ship with sensible defaults — click the tool button on the right to view or restore them.

chat.title: Default chat model
chat.desc: Used when no model is manually selected on the home page.

optimize.title: Prompt optimization
optimize.desc: The “Optimize prompt” button in the chat input uses this model to rewrite your draft more clearly and professionally.

title.title: Title generation
title.desc: Reads recent messages after the first reply to auto-generate a conversation title.

translate.title: Translation
translate.desc: The translate button under an AI reply uses this model and prompt.

suggestion.title: Suggested replies
suggestion.desc: Generates 3–5 clickable suggestions after a reply completes.

compress.title: Context compression
compress.desc: Keeps key context when compressing long conversations and writes it back.

ocr.title: OCR
ocr.desc: A fallback image-to-text channel for chat models without vision input.

image.title: Image generation
image.desc: Entry point for models with image generation capability.

mcp.title: MCP & extensions
mcp.subtitle: Manage MCP servers, prompt injections, lorebooks, quick messages and Agent Skills.
mcp.name: Name
mcp.inject_depth_msg: Inject depth (counting back from the latest message)

server.detail_desc: When enabled, it connects and syncs tools/list automatically; the home-page MCP picker decides whether the current assistant uses it.
server.url_desc: The Streamable HTTP / SSE endpoint of the MCP server.
server.headers_desc: For auth or custom headers, as an array of key-value pairs.
server.tools_ph: Auto-filled with the tools/list result after enabling and saving; you can also edit the enable field manually
server.tools_desc: Tools are synced when auto-saving an enabled MCP service.
server.tools_empty: Tools are synced automatically once enabled and saved.

mcp.keywords_label: Keywords (press Enter to add; must match the context to trigger when “always active” is off)
mcp.keywords_disabled_ph: No keywords needed when always active
mcp.keywords_ph: Type a keyword and press Enter
mcp.no_entries: No entries yet. Click “Add entry” to start.

lorebook.desc_ph: (optional) describe what this lorebook is for

mcp.inject_content_template_ph: Injection content, supports template variables like {{cur_datetime}}
mcp.import_file_desc: Supports a single SKILL.md, or a .zip archive with multiple skills.
mcp.skill_desc_default: Describe when this skill should be used

speech.tts_subtitle: TTS providers narrate messages; ASR providers power real-time speech recognition in the input box.
speech.asr_subtitle: Supports OpenAI Realtime, DashScope and Volcengine via real-time PCM WebSocket transcription.
speech.asr_empty: No ASR service yet. Click Add to configure one.
speech.asr_card_desc: Configure auth and audio parameters for each ASR provider.
speech.name: Name
speech.silence_duration_desc: Duration used to decide the end of speech.
speech.select_asr: Select or add an ASR service.
speech.tts_empty: No TTS service yet. Click Add to configure one.
speech.tts_card_desc: Message narration uses the current provider and is logged as a request.
speech.test_title: Read a test sentence using the current config
speech.test_text: This is a test voice to verify the current TTS configuration.
speech.test_system_done: Test text played via system voice
speech.system_pitch_desc: System voice pitch can't be adjusted directly; this setting is exported with the config only.
speech.select_tts: Select or add a TTS service.

data.extracting: Upload done, extracting and importing data...
data.pack_slow: With many attachments, packing may take tens of seconds. Please wait.
data.extract_slow: Parsing chat history from a large backup may take a few minutes. Don't close the app.
data.no_schema_warn: Note: the phone-side database schema isn't registered yet, so backups won't include conversations. Export a backup under “Data backup” to register the schema.
data.restore_confirm: Restoring {{name}} overwrites your current local settings, conversations and logs. Continue?
data.s3_restore_confirm: Restore from the remote backup {{name}}? This overwrites the current local state.
data.register_note: Note: this needs to be done once on first use. Re-register when the phone app updates.
data.export_done:
Backup exported: {{name}}
Find it in your browser's default download folder.
data.import_confirm: Importing overwrites the current local settings, conversations and logs. Continue?
data.import_network_error: Network error — is the backend service running?
data.export_with_schema: This export includes {{count}} conversations and all settings, ready to import on the phone.
data.export_without_schema: The phone-side schema isn't registered, so the backup won't include conversations. Register it under “Android compatibility” above first.
data.title: Data
data.subtitle: Local import/export, chat file storage, and WebDAV / S3 remote sync.
data.compat_summary: Phone-side database schema registered (version {{version}}, hash {{hash}}...). PC exports are recognized by the phone.
data.unregistered_warn: The phone-side database schema isn't registered. PC conversations can't be imported to the phone, and S3 / WebDAV backups won't include conversations.
data.current_format: Current format: version {{version}}, hash {{hash}}.... To update, re-upload a phone backup.
data.step1: On the phone, open Rikkahub → Settings → Backup and export a .zip backup
data.step2: Click the button below and pick that .zip to upload
data.step3: The PC extracts only the schema (no data is imported to PC)
data.backup_desc: Export a .zip (Android-compatible) containing settings, conversations, files, Skills, MCP / prompt injections / lorebooks / quick messages. Import also accepts Android .zip and legacy PC .json. Large backups may take 1-2 minutes.
data.chat_files_desc: Uploaded avatars and attachments are saved to the local data directory and served via /api/files.
data.webdav_desc: Test the connection, back up now, list remote backups, restore or delete. Backups include the local JSON state, Skills and uploaded files.
data.no_remote_backups: No remote backups. Click “Refresh” to read the WebDAV directory.
data.no_remote_backups_s3: No remote backups. Click “Refresh” to read the S3 bucket.
data.s3_desc: Standard AWS S3 and compatible endpoints (MinIO/R2/Tencent COS/Alibaba OSS, etc.). Backup contents match WebDAV.
